title: Research
text:
Research
  
    Research
  

  
    Linked Data, Semantic Integration and Digital Twins
    Human Behaviour and Energy Use
    Urban Planning and the Built Environment
  

  
    
      01
      
        Linked Data, Semantic Integration and Digital Twins
        Linked data, ontology and digital twin methods for integrating urban information across sectors and spatial scales.
      
    

    
        
          
          
            2026Sustainable Cities and Society
            Multi-scale urban energy data integration for energy poverty assessment: A semantic approach with evidence from the Netherlands
            Integrates building and neighbourhood energy data through the Neighbourhood Energy Ontology and machine learning, supporting transparent, traceable energy poverty assessment across spatial scales in the Netherlands.
            Digital twinLinked dataEnergy poverty
          
        
        
          
          
            2025Journal of Physics: Conference Series
            Bridging Data Silos in Electric Vehicle Charging: Developing the EVCA Ontology to Enhance Interoperability
            Develops the EVCA ontology to connect vehicle, charging, mobility and neighbourhood data, reducing data silos and improving interoperability for urban electric vehicle charging analysis and planning.
          
        
        
          
          
            2025Applied Energy
            Building sustainable urban energy systems: The role of linked data in photovoltaic generation estimation at neighbourhood level
            Integrates heterogeneous urban datasets with linked data and Semantic Web technologies to estimate neighbourhood photovoltaic generation, improving the traceability and interoperability of renewable energy analysis for urban planning.
            Solar energySemantic web technologyLinked data
          
        
        
          
          
            2025Digital Engineering
            Reflecting City Digital Twins (CDTs) for sustainable urban development: Roles, challenges and directions
            Reviews the roles, architecture and implementation challenges of city digital twins, clarifying how integrated urban data and digital representations can support sustainable urban governance and planning.
            City digital twinsData integrationUrban governance
          
        
    
  

  
    
      02
      
        Human Behaviour and Energy Use
        Behavioural, personality and decision making perspectives on energy use in homes, hotels and workplaces.
      
    

    
        
          
          
            2025Journal of Physics: Conference Series
            Dynamic shifts in building energy demand: an activity-based analysis of post-COVID work and lifestyle changes
            Uses activity-based urban energy modelling and scenarios to examine how post-COVID working and lifestyle changes redistribute building energy demand across space and time, informing demand-sensitive planning.
          
        
        
          
          
            2021Resources, Conservation and Recycling
            Are you an energy saver at home? The personality insights of household energy conservation behaviors based on theory of planned behavior
            Combines survey data, personality traits and the theory of planned behaviour to explain differences in household energy conservation, supporting more targeted behavioural interventions for resident groups.
            Energy-saving behaviorPersonality traitsTheory of planned behavior
          
        
        
          
          
            2023Environmental Impact Assessment Review
            Exploring the heterogeneity in drivers of energy-saving behaviours among hotel guests: Insights from the theory of planned behaviour and personality profiles
            Examines hotel guests through the theory of planned behaviour and personality profiles, identifying heterogeneous drivers of energy-saving behaviour that can inform more targeted hotel energy-management strategies.
            Energy-saving behaviourPersonality profileTheory of planned behaviour
          
        
        
          
          
            2023Applied Energy
            The willingness of social housing tenants to participate in natural gas-free heating systems project: Insights from a stated choice experiment in the Netherlands
            Uses a stated choice experiment to assess social housing tenants' participation in natural gas-free heating projects, informing household-centred decisions for the Dutch housing energy transition.
            Energy efficient renovationEnergy transitionSocial housing
          
        
    
  

  
    
      03
      
        Urban Planning and the Built Environment
        Urban planning research on wellbeing, resilience, spatial justice and the built environment.
      
    

    
        
          
          
            2026Computational Urban Science
            Understanding subjective well-being in urban emergencies: the role of time use and personality traits
            Analyses time use and personality traits to examine subjective well-being during urban emergencies, helping planners understand how activity patterns and individual differences shape residents' emergency experiences.
            Personality TraitsActivity PatternSubjective Well-Being (SWB)
          
        
        
          
          
            2023Sustainable Cities and Society
            Community resilience in city emergency: Exploring the roles of environmental perception, social justice and community attachment in subjective well-being of vulnerable residents
            Examines environmental perception, social justice and community attachment among vulnerable residents, clarifying how these factors inform community resilience and human-centred urban emergency planning practice and policy.
            Social justiceCommunity resilienceVulnerable group
          
        
        
          
          
            2025Developments in the Built Environment
            Towards a human-centric city emergency response: Modelling activity patterns of urban population
            Models urban population activity patterns to support a human-centred understanding of emergency response, helping planners assess how everyday movements shape demands on city systems during disruptions.
          
        
        
          
          
            Available at SSRN 6137116
            Beyond the Office: A Systematic Review of Changing Working Patterns and Their Urban Footprints
            Synthesises evidence on changing working patterns and their effects on workplaces, housing, mobility and urban form, providing a planning perspective on the spatial consequences of flexible work.
            Working patternsRemote workBuilt environment
          
        
    
  
  View complete publication list →

title: Research
text:
Research
  
    Research and publications
    Research
    My research examines linked data, semantic integration, urban energy transition, neighbourhood scale analysis, building and transport energy systems, renewables, housing, and social equity. The themes below organise the current research profile reflected across this website and group related publications from the current publication list.
  

  
    Linked data
    Urban energy
    Renewable energy
    EV charging
    Housing equity
    Digital built environment
  

  
    
      01
      
        Linked data, semantic integration and knowledge graphs
        This theme focuses on linked data, Semantic Web technologies, and ontology-based modelling for organising heterogeneous urban energy data. It addresses differences in format, scale, terminology, and institutional origin.
        Related publications
        
          Liu, Xuan; Yang, Dujuan; de Vries, Bauke; Feng, Zhaorong; Wang, Qian-Cheng (2025). Bridging Data Silos in Electric Vehicle Charging: Developing the EVCA Ontology to Enhance Interoperability. Journal of Physics: Conference Series, 3140(6), 062024.
          Liu, Xuan; Yang, Dujuan; de Vries, Bauke (2022). Supporting Site Selection Decision-Making Process for Public Charging Station of Electric Vehicles using Semantic Web Technologies. Urban Transitions 2022: Integrating urban and transport planning, environment and health for healthier urban living.
        
      
    

    
      02
      
        Urban energy transition and decision support
        This theme connects sustainable urban energy systems with digital decision support for planning. It examines how urban energy data can support clearer interpretation of local energy conditions and transition pathways.
        Related publications
        
          Xu, Qian; Tang, Yun; Wang, Qian-Cheng; Liu, Xuan; others (2024). Personalised Green Financial Derivative Promoting Household Energy-Saving and Pro-Environmental Behaviours.
          Xu, Qian; Li, Shengping; Shen, Liyin; Chang, Ruidong; Wang, Qian-Cheng; Liu, Xuan; Chen, Yang (2023). Pricing strategy for household energy-saving option (HESO): A novel option-based intervention for promoting household energy efficiency. Environmental Impact Assessment Review, 98, 106969.
          Wang, Qian-Cheng; Ren, Yi-Tian; Liu, Xuan; Chang, Rui-Dong; Zuo, Jian (2023). Exploring the heterogeneity in drivers of energy-saving behaviours among hotel guests: Insights from the theory of planned behaviour and personality profiles. Environmental Impact Assessment Review, 99, 107012.
          Wang, Qian-Cheng; Lou, Yi-Ning; Liu, Xuan; Jin, Xin; Li, Xuewei; Xu, Qian (2023). Determinants and mechanisms driving energy-saving behaviours of long-stay hotel guests: Comparison of leisure, business and extended-stay residential cases. Energy Reports, 9, 1354--1365.
          Yu, Chuan-Rui; Liu, Xuan; Wang, Qian-Cheng; Yang, Dujuan (2023). Solving the comfort-retrofit conundrum through post-occupancy evaluation and multi-objective optimisation. Building Services Engineering Research \& Technology, 44(4), 381--403.
          Li, Yi-Bin; Wang, Tian-Yuan; Lin, Rui-Xin; Yu, Si-Nan; Liu, Xuan; Wang, Qian-Cheng; Xu, Qian (2022). Behaviour-driven energy-saving in hotels: The roles of extraversion and past behaviours on guests闁?energy-conservation intention. Buildings, 12(7), 941.
          Wang, Qian-Cheng; Chang, Ruidong; Xu, Qian; Liu, Xuan; Jian, Izzy Yi; Ma, Yao-Tian; Wang, Yi-Xuan (2021). The impact of personality traits on household energy conservation behavioral intentions--an empirical study based on theory of planned behavior in Xi'an. Sustainable Energy Technologies and Assessments, 43, 100949.
          Liu, Xuan; Wang, Qian-Cheng; Jian, Izzy Yi; Chi, Hung-Lin; Yang, Dujuan; Chan, Edwin Hon-Wan (2021). Are you an energy saver at home? The personality insights of household energy conservation behaviors based on theory of planned behavior. Resources, Conservation and Recycling, 174, 105823.
          Wang, Qian-Cheng; Xie, Ke-Xin; Liu, Xuan; Shen, Geoffrey Qi Ping; Wei, Hsi-Hsien; Liu, Tian-Yi (2021). Psychological drivers of hotel guests闁?energy-saving behaviours闁炽儲鎽梞pirical research based on the extended theory of planned behaviour. Buildings, 11(9), 401.
          Liu, Xuan; Wang, Qiancheng; Wei, Hsi-Hsien; Chi, Hung-Lin; Ma, Yaotian; Jian, Izzy Yi (2020). Psychological and demographic factors affecting household energy-saving intentions: a TPB-based study in Northwest China. Sustainability, 12(3), 836.
          Wang, Qian-Cheng; Wang, Yi-Xuan; Jian, Izzy Yi; Wei, Hsi-Hsien; Liu, Xuan; Ma, Yao-Tian (2020). Exploring the 闁炽儲绔糿ergy-saving personality traits闁?in the office and household situation: an empirical Study. Energies, 13(14), 3535.
          Xuan, Liu; Yi, Jian Izzy; Qian-Cheng, Wang; Long-Li, Zhou; Qiao-Peng, Xie (2020). The Impacts of Big Five Personality Traits on Household Energy Conservation Behavior: A Preliminary Study in Xi闁炽儲鐛宯 China. Proceedings of the 2020 International Conference on Resource Sustainability: Sustainable Urbanisation in the BRI Era (icRS Urbanisation 2020), 59.
        
      
    

    
      03
      
        Renewable energy and neighbourhood-scale analysis
        This theme examines the neighbourhood scale as a level between individual buildings and the wider city. It supports comparison of renewable generation potential, local energy conditions, building characteristics, mobility demand, and infrastructure needs.
        Related publications
        
          Liu, Xuan; Yang, Dujuan; Donkers, Alex; de Vries, Bauke (2025). Building sustainable urban energy systems: The role of linked data in photovoltaic generation estimation at neighbourhood level. Applied Energy, 378, 124749.
          Liu, Xuan; Yang, Dujuan; de Vries, Bauke; Wang, Qiancheng (2023). Using Linked Data to Assist in Estimating Hourly PV Power Generation at the Neighbourhood Level: A Case Study in the Netherlands.
        
      
    

    
      04
      
        EV charging, mobility and cross-sector interaction
        This theme addresses cross sector energy interactions, especially the relation between EV charging demand, photovoltaic availability, building energy conditions, and local infrastructure. It supports analysis of temporal mismatch, spatial variation, and scenario-based planning.
        Related publications
        
          Wang, Qian-Cheng; Feng, Zhao-Rong; Liu, Xuan (2025). Dynamic shifts in building energy demand: an activity-based analysis of post-COVID work and lifestyle changes. Journal of Physics: Conference Series, 3140(6), 062013.
          Liu, Xuan; Yang, Dujuan; de Vries, Bauke (2021). How to integrate electric vehicle into urban energy system?-the data structure for integrating urban building, electric vehicles, and land use with linked data approach. International Conference on Sustainable Technology and Development.
        
      
    

    
      05
      
        Housing, wellbeing and social equity
        This theme connects data spaces, housing, urban decision support, explainable AI, and housing-related inequalities. It also relates to the ways vulnerable populations navigate urban systems.
        Related publications
        
          Liu, Xuan; Xu, Qian; Feng, Zhaorong; Jian, Yi Izzy; Wang, Qian-Cheng (2026). Understanding subjective well-being in urban emergencies: the role of time use and personality traits. Computational Urban Science, 6(1), 3.
          Wang, Qian-Cheng; He, Ping; Li, Yibin; Hou, Yuting; Jian, Yi Izzy; Liu, Xuan (2025). Towards a human-centric city emergency response: Modelling activity patterns of urban population. Developments in the Built Environment, 21, 100633.
          Wang, Qian-Cheng; Liu, Xuan; Jian, Izzy Yi; Zhang, En-Jia; Hou, Yu-Ting; Siu, Kin Wai Michael; Li, Yi-Bin (2023). Community resilience in city emergency: Exploring the roles of environmental perception, social justice and community attachment in subjective well-being of vulnerable residents. Sustainable Cities and Society, 97, 104745.
          Liu, Xuan; Yang, Dujuan; Arentze, Theo; Wielders, Tom (2023). The willingness of social housing tenants to participate in natural gas-free heating systems project: Insights from a stated choice experiment in the Netherlands. Applied Energy, 350, 121706.
          Jian, Izzy Yi; Chan, Edwin HW; Luo, May Jiemei; Yao, Terry Yepeng; Wang, Qian-Cheng; Liu, Xuan (2021). A spatial justice study of pedestrian-friendly routes to privately owned public open space in Hong Kong. ISUF 2020 Virtual Conference Proceedings.
          Xiang, Liqun; Shen, Geoffrey QP; Tan, Yongtao; Liu, Xuan (2020). Emerging evolution trends of studies on age-friendly cities and communities: a scientometric review. Ageing \& Society, 1--31.
          Feng, Zhaorong; Liu, Xuan; Jian, Yi Izzy; Bao, Helen XH; Tsou, Jin Yeu; Wang, Qian-Cheng. Building Urban Resilience Through Nature: A Systematic Review of Urban Green Spaces as Nature-based Solutions. Available at SSRN 6177981.
          Zhang, Kexin; Liu, Xuan; Bao, Helen XH; Wang, Wenbin; Wang, Qian-Cheng. Beyond the Office: A Systematic Review of Changing Working Patterns and Their Urban Footprints. Available at SSRN 6137116.
        
      
    

    
      06
      
        Digital built environment and infrastructure applications
        This theme covers applications of digital urban evidence, city digital twins, BIM, construction safety, infrastructure analysis, and knowledge graph-based approaches in the built environment.
        Related publications
        
          Wang, Qian-Cheng; Sun, Maoran; Liu, Xuan; Tao, Fei; Yang, Dujuan; Bardhan, Ronita (2025). Reflecting City Digital Twins (CDTs) for sustainable urban development: Roles, challenges and directions. Digital Engineering, 5, 100035.
          Li, Xuewei; Li, Shuchen; Yuan, Jingfeng; Wan, Zeen; Liu, Xuan (2025). A data-driven and knowledge graph-based research on safety risk-coupled evolution analysis and assessment in shield tunneling. Tunnelling and Underground Space Technology, 162, 106657.
          Li, Xuewei; Yuan, Jingfeng; Liu, Xuan; Wang, Guangqi; Wang, Qian-Cheng (2024). The effects of joint-contract functions on PPP project value creation: a mediation model. Engineering, construction and architectural management, 31(10), 4162--4183.
          Xu, Qian; Hwang, Bon-Gang; Choo, Rui Qi; Zheng, Xian; Kong, Lei; Wang, Qian-Cheng; Liu, Xuan (2023). Comparison of construction project risks before and during COVID-19 in Singapore: criticality and management strategies. Construction Management and Economics, 41(10), 875--891.
          Wang, Qian-Cheng; Yu, Si-Nan; Chen, Zi-Xiao; Weng, Yi-Wei; Xue, Jin; Liu, Xuan (2023). Promoting additive construction in fast-developing areas: An analysis of policies and stakeholder perspectives. Developments in the Built Environment, 16, 100271.
          Li, Xuewei; Yang, Dujuan; Yuan, Jingfeng; Donkers, Alex; Liu, Xuan (2022). BIM-enabled semantic web for automated safety checks in subway construction. Automation in construction, 141, 104454.
          Li, Xuewei; Yuan, Jingfeng; Liu, Xuan; Ke, Yongjian; Jia, Sijia (2022). Identifying critical influencing factors of the value creation of urban rail transit PPP projects in China. Buildings, 12(8), 1080.

title: Xuan Liu
text:
Xuan Liu
  
    
      
      Xuan Liu
      Department of Land EconomyUniversity of CambridgeCambridge, United Kingdom

      
        Urban planning
        Linked data
        Semantic integration
        Urban energy transition
      

      
        Email
        Google Scholar
        LinkedIn
        Scopus
        ResearchGate
      
    
  

  
    
      Greeting and welcome to my website 😀
      I am a Research Fellow in the Department of Land Economy, the University of Cambridge.
      My research connects semantic data integration with urban energy transition and decision support. I am particularly interested in urban energy transitions across different spatial, sectoral, institutional, and planning contexts.
      Thank you for visiting. I am always happy to exchange ideas, explore shared research interests, and develop new collaborations.      
    
    
      From urban data to planning evidence
      
        
          01
          
          Structure urban evidence
          Organise diverse urban data into interconnected and interpretable evidence.
          Buildings, mobility, time use, socio-economic conditions and 3D city information
        
        
          02
          
          Reveal cross-sector relationships
          Analyse spatial and temporal relationships between building, transport, energy and society.
          Semantic integration, spatio-temporal modelling, advanced statistics and explainable AI
        
        
          03
          
          Support place-based decisions
          Translate analysis into evidence for local planning and sustainable urban development.
          Energy transition, renewable integration, infrastructure planning, housing and social equity
        
      
    

    
      Research interests
      
        linked data
        semantic integration
        city digital twin
        social justice
        data spaces
      
    

    
      Research themes
      
        
          Semantic integration for urban energy transition
          Linked data and ontology-based modelling for organising heterogeneous urban energy data across sectors, scales and institutional sources.
        
        
          Neighbourhood level energy analysis
          Analysis of local energy conditions, renewable generation potential, building characteristics, mobility demand and infrastructure needs.
        
        
          Building, transport and renewable energy interactions
          Research on cross sector energy interactions, including EV charging demand, photovoltaic availability, building energy conditions and local infrastructure.
        
        
          Urban data spaces
          Work on data spaces, urban decision support, digital urban evidence.
        
      
    

    
      Recent updates
